metadata.seed: I've just been feeling kind of in a rut. I mean, I've been looking for a job and haven't really gotten anywhere. And then I had a drink the other night, and I just felt like me not having a job and me going out and having that drink, it wasn't deserved. And I just felt like such a loser because that was the time that I should have been at work if I had a job, and instead I was at the bar drinking. So I had one drink, and then it led to another. Cause then I just felt worse and I figured I had one, so.

patient.query: I've just been feeling kind of in a rut. I mean, I've been looking for a job and haven't really gotten anywhere. And I just felt like such a loser because that was the time that I should have been at work if I had a job, and instead I was at the bar drinking. So I had one drink, and then it led to another. Cause then I just felt worse and I figured I had one, so.

I should have been at work

patient.query: I don't know, I guess I imagine being more confident, like being able to speak up in meetings or even just being okay with who I am. But then I worry that if I try, it’ll just end in embarrassment or disappointment. It makes me want to just... stay quiet.

automatic_thoughts: if I try, it’ll end in embarrassment
if I try, it’ll end in disappointment

patient.query: Yeah, I mean, I’ve had moments where I tried to be more open or take a risk, and it didn’t go well. Like, there was this time I spoke up in a group, and I felt so stupid afterward. It’s like that moment just sticks with me, you know? So now I think, why bother? What if I just end up feeling worse again?

automatic_thoughts: What if I just end up feeling worse again?

I felt like I just... ruined the moment or said something dumb. It’s like, instead of being proud of myself for trying, I just felt embarrassed. It’s just hard to shake that feeling off.

I remember thinking, “See, this is what happens when you speak up—no one cares.” It felt really isolating, like I was just... invisible. I just wanted to sink into my chair and hope no one remembered what I said.

patient.query: I guess it’s just this feeling I’ve had for a long time. Like, sometimes I feel like I don’t matter in conversations or even in groups. It’s like I’m just there, but my voice doesn’t really count. When things like that happen, it just kind of reinforces that belief, and I can’t help but think they’d prefer if I didn’t say anything at all.

automatic_thoughts: they’d prefer if I didn’t say anything

patient.query: It’s like a storm in my head, honestly. I start second-guessing everything, even what I think I want to say. Sometimes I tell myself it’s better to just keep quiet, that it’s easier that way. And then I feel this sinking feeling, like I’m missing out on something important, but the fear just keeps holding me back. It’s frustrating.
